### tail

>  tail (s:str|None, limit:int=40000)

*Return the tail of a long string, truncating if it exceeds limit.*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>

<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>s</td>
<td>str | None</td>
<td></td>
<td>String to potentially truncate.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>limit</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>40000</td>
<td>Maximum length before truncation (default 40000).</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>str</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Original string if under limit, otherwise truncated with
indicator.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

### ok

>  ok (errcode:int)

*Return True if the given error code indicates success (zero).*

<thead>
<tr>

<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>

## Environment Wrapping

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/subprocess.py#L86"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### wrap_with_env

>  wrap_with_env (cmd:List[str], project:pathlib.Path, use_env:bool=True,
>                     pkg_mgrs:Optional[List[str]]=None)

*Wrap a command to run inside the project’s conda/mamba environment.*

If use_env is True and the project has an associated conda/mamba env,
prefix the command with ‘mamba/conda run -n <env_name>’.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>cmd</td>
<td>List</td>
<td></td>
<td>Command to wrap.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>project</td>
<td>Path</td>
<td></td>
<td>Path to the nbdev project root.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>use_env</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>If True, attempt to wrap with environment activation.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>pkg_mgrs</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Package managers to try, in order. Defaults to [‘mamba’, ‘conda’,
‘micromamba’].</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>List</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>The command, possibly prefixed with environment
activation.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

### run

>  run (cmd:List[str], cwd:pathlib.Path)

*Execute a subprocess command and capture its output.*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 9%" />
<col style="width: 38%" />
<col style="width: 52%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>cmd</td>
<td>List</td>
<td>Command and arguments to execute.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>cwd</td>
<td>Path</td>
<td>Working directory for the command.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Dict</strong></td>
<td><strong>Dictionary with keys:<br><br>- cmd: The command as a quoted
string<br>- cwd: Working directory path<br>- returncode: Process return
code<br>- stdout: Standard output (truncated if too long)<br>- stderr:
Standard error (truncated if too long)<br>- ok: True if returncode is
zero</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
